description: Teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi made history by becoming the youngest player to reach 500 Indian Premier League runs and the fastest by balls faced. description: Akshay Kumar's latest horror-comedy, 'Bhooth Bangla', is making waves at the box office, raking in over Rs 100 crore globally within its first four days. Although it faced a slight decline during the weekdays, the film still attracted audiences, collecting Rs 6.15 crore on Day 6 in India and pushing its total net earnings to Rs 78.90 crore. Read on to know more in detail.

description: A man was allegedly murdered by his wife, daughters, and minor son in Mahoba district. The family feared he would misuse Rs 82,500 arranged for his daughter's wedding. The body was found in a drain. The accused confessed to strangling him and dumping the body. Police recovered the murder weapon, cash, and a damaged mobile phone.

description: A 23-year-old man, accused of a brutal rape and murder in Delhi, allegedly committed another heinous crime in Alwar, Rajasthan, hours prior. After $6.25 bn 'gift' to 'Trump Accounts', Michael & Susan Dell donate $750 m
description: Michael and Susan Dell have pledged $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin for a new medical center and research campus. This significant donation will focus on integrating AI and advanced computing to improve patient care, research, and education. The project, expected to open by 2030, aims to enhance Austin's healthcare capabilities and advance medical innovation.

title: 'We are installing tracking software in your machines': Meta to US employees
description: Meta is installing new tracking software on US employees' computers to capture mouse movements, clicks, and keystrokes for AI training. This initiative aims to improve AI agents' ability to perform work tasks autonomously, like using shortcuts. Meta assures employees the data won't be used for performance reviews, only for enhancing AI models.

title: 1 lakh fake ENO, 50k coffee sachets seized: FSSAI shares 2 simple purity tests
description: Authorities have busted two illegal manufacturing units in Delhi, seizing over 1.5 lakh counterfeit sachets of popular brands like ENO and Nescafé. This crackdown highlights the growing problem of fake and adulterated food products in India, which poses a significant risk to consumers. The report also offers simple methods to detect common coffee adulterants like clay and chicory powder.
